text: Fill in the missing anatomical terms to complete these paragraphs about the axilla, brachial plexus, and shoulder region.
questions: The axilla is a *pyramid/pyramidal*-shaped space. Its anterior wall is formed by the *pectoralis major* and pectoralis minor. The posterior wall contains *subscapularis*, latissimus dorsi, and teres major. The medial wall has the ribs and *serratus anterior*.

The brachial plexus arises from ventral rami of *C5/C5-T1*-T1. Injury to the upper trunk (C5-C6) causes *Erb's/Erbs* palsy with the waiter's tip position. Injury to the lower trunk causes *Klumpke's/Klumpkes* paralysis with claw hand.

The rotator cuff muscles are remembered by the mnemonic *SITS*. The most commonly torn muscle is *supraspinatus*. The serratus anterior is innervated by the *long thoracic/long thoracic nerve* nerve — its paralysis causes *winging/winging of scapula* of the scapula.

The axillary artery is divided into three parts by *pectoralis minor*. The number of branches matches the part number: *1*, 2, and 3 branches respectively. The *axillary/axillary nerve* nerve wraps around the surgical neck of the humerus.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the axilla and shoulder anatomy.
Good effort! Check the details you missed.
